---
title: "Flannel Weed (Sida cordifolia)"
slug: "sida-cordifolia"
group: "Plantae"
image: "https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/475168583/medium.jpg"        # keep for thumbnails
image-alt: "Flannel Weed"  # keep for a11y
tax_kingdom: "Plantae"
tax_class: "Magnoliopsida"
tax_order: "Malvales"
tax_family: "Malvaceae"
categories: ["Plantae", "Magnoliopsida", "Malvales", "Malvaceae"]   # these drive the chips + filtering
freeze: true
---

<figure class="species-figure">

<img src="https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/475168583/medium.jpg" alt="Flannel Weed" class="species-photo"/>

<figcaption class="photo-credit">

Photo: <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/people/damien6868">
damien6868</a> (CC BY-NC) via <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/observations/200910713">iNaturalist</a>

</figcaption>

</figure>

## Taxonomy

-   **Class:** Magnoliopsida
-   **Order:** Malvales
-   **Family:** Malvaceae

## Occurrence at DeLuca Preserve

-   [DeLuca Bio Blitz Species Observations](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?project_id=191727&subview=map&taxon_id=132448)

## Conservation & rarity context

-   IUCN: Not Listed
-   **Rarity note:** Common species in Florida found in disturbed areas.

## Natural history notes

A herbaceous plant characterized by its soft, velvety (flannel-like) leaves, heart-shaped foliage, and small pale yellow to orange flowers. In Florida, it is commonly found in disturbed areas such as roadsides, fields, and pastures. It grows during the warmer months and can spread readily, often behaving as a weedy species in open habitats.

## References / links

-   [iNaturalist species page](https://www.inaturalist.org/taxa/132448-Sida-cordifolia)
-   [Local profile](https://mrec.ifas.ufl.edu/resources/weedsbyflowercolor/yellow/sidacordifolia/)
